pi_items.source: Official DreamPlan file extension documentation from multiple file extension databases (fileinfo.com, file-extensions.org, solvusoft.com, filext.com confirm .ddp is the standard DreamPlan project file extension). NCH Software official sources consistently reference .ddp, not .dpp.

Official product name from NCH Software (nchsoftware.com). Environment configuration file (env.json) confirms 'NCH DreamPlan Home Design Software'. Web search results consistently reference the full product name.

DreamPlan feature documentation from multiple sources confirm the presence of: (1) 'Walls tool' for drawing walls/partitions, (2) 'Doors tool' for adding doors to walls, (3) Furniture library with interior design items including storage pieces (shelves, wardrobes, closet rods). Task description also explicitly requires these tools.

Task description requirement: 'Place at least one storage item (shelving, wardrobe, or closet rod) inside the new closet space (found under Furniture > Bedroom > Storage)'. Verifier.py also checks for storage_item_visible.

Environment setup scripts and task configuration consistently reference C:\Users\Docker\Documents\ as the project directory. This is the default Windows user Documents folder for the Docker user account defined in env.json.

privileged_info_summary: DreamPlan Home Design Software is an NCH Software application used for architectural and interior design. The task requires constructing a walk-in closet using the Wall tool to create partition walls, Door tool to add access, and Furniture tools to place at least one storage item. The official file format for DreamPlan projects is .ddp (not .dpp as claimed in this task). Projects are saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\. The task includes verification that the project file is saved with a minimum file size of 1000 bytes, walls are created, a door is installed, and storage is added.
